last_completed_round.direction_name: Reuse one 16x16 epilogue scratch tile per warp with warp-synchronous pair stores
last_completed_round.hypothesis: Round 3 reduced the B-side bank pressure, but active warps still sit around 33% and the kernel still reserves 12.8 KB of shared memory per block, with most of the non-staging footprint coming from the three-tile-per-warp float `c_shared` epilogue buffer. Reusing a single 16x16 scratch tile per warp, then writing each MMA tile back immediately with warp-synchronous coordination, should lower the shared-memory footprint and the post-MMA shared round-trip without changing the main tensor-core loop.

notes: Build passed for round 5/20. Node A will measure the result next.
